rows.name: Economic Update
rows.cadence: monthly · small, small, BIG (quarterly, on APS releases)

rows.name: Local Economic Assessment
rows.cadence: annual · December (2026 adds a one-off interim)

topics.name: Economy
topics.note: ONS topic · regional accounts, GDP, GVA, prices

topics.name: People, population and community
topics.note: ONS topic · population, housing, deprivation, health

topics.name: Policy
topics.note: Briefings · government & regional policy for the local economy

products.title: Gateshead Productivity & GVA
products.description: The borough's £5bn economy — GVA in current prices and real terms, output per hour and per job, sectoral structure and concentration risk. Benchmarked against the North East, the UK and Gateshead's ONS economic statistical neighbours, with NOMIS business and employment counts. Built to absorb the imminent 2024 data.
products.sources: ONS balanced GVA & subregional productivity · NOMIS

products.title: GDHI — Gateshead & the North East
products.description: Household income in one place. Official GDHI per head benchmarking, 1997–2023 — £19,127 in 2023, £5,709 below the UK average, 329th of 361 UK local authorities, vs the NECA seven and statistical neighbours — plus the small-area explorer across 3,427 UK areas (2010–2023): choropleth of all 126 Gateshead LSOAs, inequality ratios and full income-component breakdowns.
products.sources: ONS GDHI estimates · ELS regional GDHI per head 1997–2023

products.title: ELS scorecard — all local indicators
products.description: Every ONS Explore Local Statistics indicator covering Gateshead — 88 indicators across 10 domains — ranked against all UK local authorities and benchmarked vs England/UK, the North East, NECA and statistical neighbours. Sortable scorecard plus per-indicator Explore view with trends, CIs and distributions.
products.sources: ONS Explore Local Statistics · 88 indicators · 10 domains

products.title: Gateshead Business Map
products.description: 11,000+ companies registered in Gateshead borough, mapped from Companies House and enriched with IMD 2025 deprivation, ONS sector churn, FSA food premises, BRES jobs and UKRI innovation. Intervention & investment scores per LSOA and ward, high-streets lens on Local Plan centres, plus sector saturation.
products.sources: Companies House · IMD 2025 · ONS · FSA · UKRI · rebuilt monthly

products.title: North East Creative Industries (CCIS)
products.description: DCMS creative sector business counts, employment and GVA across the seven North East Combined Authority local authorities. Companion to the CCIS Framework Data & Evidence consultation.
products.sources: DCMS Creative Industries Sector Economic Estimates

products.title: Gateshead Built-Up Areas
products.description: Profile of the seven built-up areas inside Gateshead borough — Gateshead, Whickham, Blaydon, Birtley, Ryton, Crawcrook & Greenside, Rowlands Gill. Population, house prices, employment, visitor economy, benchmarked against 1,395 England & Wales BUAs.

products.title: Gateshead Deprivation — IMD 2025
products.description: English Indices of Deprivation 2025 for Gateshead. All 22 wards and 126 neighbourhoods ranked, the seven domains ranked by national standing, an interactive deprivation map, and a social-value targeting matrix that separates depth of need from reach. Includes the supplementary IDACI / IDAOPI indices.

products.title: North East Employer Skills — ESS 2024
products.description: DfE Employer Skills Survey 2024: how North East employers compare with England's nine regions on vacancies, skills gaps, training, apprenticeships, future skill needs and AI. The North East tops England on apprenticeship engagement (38%) and upskilling need (68%) but sits bottom on AI adoption (11%). Regional pack — no Gateshead-level data, North East used as proxy.

products.title: Policy Update — July 2026
products.description: What changed in government and regional policy for Gateshead's economy — second edition, scan run 13 July 2026: the Northern Growth Strategy views window closing 31 July (£120m, proposed Newcastle–Gateshead MDZ), Gateshead named a North East Pathfinder partner, the new North East LSIP 2026–2029, the Industrial Strategy quarterly and year-one reports (8 July), the April 2026 Universal Credit structural break, and the Transformed LFS transition timetable under assessment. Reads like a document; replaces the 3 July edition.
products.sources: Briefing · HTML · gov.uk · parliament.uk · ONS · NE MSA
